executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with over 100 Canadian organizations victimized in the past 48 hours across all sectors. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (hit twice by separate gangs), National Money Mart (database leaked), Markham Stouffville Hospital, Eastern Townships School Board, and Peterborough Public Health. The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security has issued an alert for critical Cisco ASA and FTD vulnerabilities (CVE-2025-20333, CVE-2025-20362, CVE-2025-20363) requiring immediate patching. The Qilin ransomware group alone has claimed 17 Canadian victims, while the Clop gang has published 14 organizations and Play gang has hit 9 targets.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is experiencing a severe ransomware crisis with over 100 organizations victimized in 48 hours across healthcare, education, public sector, financial services, and critical infrastructure. The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) has issued Alert AL25-012 for critical Cisco ASA/FTD vulnerabilities requiring immediate patching. Critical public services are disrupted including Markham Stouffville Hospital, Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, and Municipality of North Perth.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose hit twice by separate ransomware gangs (CoinbaseCartel and ShinyHunters); Active Green + Ross, Kids & Company victimized by Sinobi gang
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered database leak via Everest ransomware gang; Morison Insurance Brokers hit by Sinobi gang

report_2w.executive_summary: CISA has flagged 8 actively exploited vulnerabilities including three critical Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager flaws (CVE-2026-20122, CVE-2026-20128, CVE-2026-20133) with federal remediation deadlines in April-May 2026. North Korean Lazarus Group is blamed for a massive $290 million KelpDAO cryptocurrency heist using node compromise and DDoS tactics. Canada Life suffered a data breach exposing 5.6 million Salesforce records after ShinyHunters threat group compromised employee accounts, with an extortion deadline set for April 21. Over 6,000 publicly exposed Apache ActiveMQ instances remain vulnerable to CVE-2026-34197, presenting immediate risk.

items.name: Lazarus Group (North Korean APT)
items.targets: KelpDAO cryptocurrency platform ($290M theft), C-level executives globally
items.ttps: T1189 (Drive-by Compromise), T1498 (DDoS), T1556 (Modify Authentication Process), credential theft malware deployment, node infrastructure compromise

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Canada Life (5.6 million Salesforce records)
items.ttps: T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage), employee account compromise, extortion with deadline

items.name: Seedworm (Iranian APT)
items.targets: Global organizations via Microsoft Teams
items.ttps: T1566.002 (Phishing: Spearphishing Link), T1078 (Valid Accounts), Microsoft Teams-based attacks

items.name: Akira Ransomware
items.targets: Arctic Home Living
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), double extortion tactics

items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), double extortion tactics

items.ttps: T1584 (Compromise Infrastructure), T1498 (DDoS), proxy operations for Iranian government

items.name: Vidar Information Stealer

canada_landscape.summary: Canada Life suffered a significant breach exposing 5.6 million Salesforce records after ShinyHunters compromised employee accounts, with an extortion deadline of April 21, 2026. No CCCS advisories were identified in this reporting period. Multiple articles discuss Canadian geopolitical and economic topics but no additional cybersecurity incidents targeting Canadian critical infrastructure were reported.
canada_landscape.financial: Canada Life Assurance — data breach exposing 5.6 million Salesforce records via employee account compromise by ShinyHunters threat group
